description: You are a microscopy technician performing quality control on a batch of fluorescence images in `~/Fiji_Data/raw/BBBC005/`. Assess the focus quality of all 'w1' (nuclear stain) TIF images by calculating four metrics: Variance of Laplacian (VoL) (3x3 Laplacian kernel variance), Normalized Variance (Variance/Mean), Tenengrad (Mean of squared Sobel gradient magnitudes), and StdDev (Standard deviation of raw intensities). Rank the images by VoL from highest to lowest and classify each as 'PASS' if its VoL is greater than the batch median, or 'FAIL' otherwise. Generate a report CSV at `~/Fiji_Data/results/focus_qc/focus_metrics.csv` with columns: filename, laplacian_var, normalized_var, tenengrad, stddev, rank, and qc_status. Create a text summary at `~/Fiji_Data/results/focus_qc/qc_summary.txt` listing the total images, PASS/FAIL counts, best/worst focused filenames, and the median threshold used. Save a visual comparison montage of the best and worst focused images to `~/Fiji_Data/results/focus_qc/focus_comparison.png`.
